name: Nsc (Naca Counseling Subsidiary) - New Orleans, La
description_short: NACA is a HUD-approved nonprofit housing counselor providing character-based mortgage lending and homeownership services to underserved communities across America.
description_long:
NACA (Neighborhood Assistance Corporation of America) was founded in 1988 by the Boston Hotel Workers Union and has spent 36 years as a nonprofit advocate for affordable homeownership and economic justice. The organization has served 3 million people, helped 500,000 homeowners achieve ownership, and facilitated $20 billion in mortgage commitments while operating 30% of all HUD housing counseling nationally.

NACA offers character-based mortgage lending that does not consider credit scores, requires no down payment, charges no closing costs or fees, and includes no mortgage insurance. Their current fixed-rate mortgages are offered at 5.625% (30-year), 5.125% (20-year), and 5% (15-year) as of April 2026. Members access services through a member portal for document uploads and status checking, and can attend in-person "Achieve The Dream" events in multiple cities to become NACA Qualified and work directly with their team.

NACA distinguishes itself through its explicit mission to close the racial wealth disparity gap and fight predatory lending practices. The organization operates as an advocacy-driven nonprofit rather than a traditional lender, campaigns against corporate landlords and Wall Street real estate investors, and actively engages members as "NACtivist" volunteers. They provide comprehensive housing counseling beyond mortgage services, address predatory rental practices, and maintain a strong community-based model centered on member participation.

While NACA's no-credit-score approach and below-market rates are compelling, consumers should understand that membership qualification requires attending multi-day events and meeting character-based lending criteria that differ substantially from traditional underwriting. The organization's focus on advocacy and community participation means their services integrate broader economic justice goals alongside mortgage lending, which may appeal to mission-aligned borrowers but represents a different business model than conventional lenders.
pros: No credit score consideration — character-based lending approved borrowers traditional lenders reject
Zero down payment, no closing costs, no fees, and no mortgage insurance required
Below-market fixed rates (5.625% 30-year as of April 2026) with rate locks for qualified members
Serves as official HUD housing counselor — 30% of all HUD counseling services nationally
Established 36-year nonprofit track record with $20 billion in mortgage commitments
Multi-city in-person events (Hartford, Little Rock, Milwaukee, Fort Myers) for direct member qualification
Comprehensive mission addressing predatory lending, corporate landlords, and wealth disparity
cons: Requires attendance at multi-day in-person 'Achieve The Dream' events to qualify — not available online
Character-based underwriting criteria unstated on website — qualification process opaque
Limited transparency on approval rates, typical loan amounts, or timeframes for funding decisions
Geographic constraint — events only in select cities, potentially requiring travel for members
No information provided on refinance options, loan servicer details, or post-closing support
best_for: Low-to-moderate income borrowers and people of color historically underserved by traditional lenders
First-time homebuyers without established credit history or prior credit damage
Mission-aligned borrowers seeking affordable homeownership through nonprofit advocacy organizations
Renters experiencing predatory landlord practices looking for alternative pathways to ownership
diagnosis: NACA is best for low-to-moderate income borrowers, first-time homebuyers, and people of color who cannot qualify through traditional credit-based lending and seek affordable homeownership through a nonprofit mission-driven organization. The main caveat is that qualification requires attending multi-day in-person events and meeting unstated character-based criteria, making this model inaccessible to those unable to travel and less transparent than conventional lenders.
services: Character-based mortgage lending without credit score consideration
In-person 'Achieve The Dream' multi-day qualification events
Member portal for document upload and application status tracking
NACtivist volunteer engagement and community organizing
Predatory lender and landlord advocacy and complaints
Lender, servicer, investor, and contractor partner network management
Financial counseling and homeownership readiness preparation
Ongoing member support and testimonial resources
typical_results_timeline: Counseling available within 1-2 weeks of contact.
address: 10555 Lake Forest Blvd Ste 3J, New Orleans, LA 70127
